title: [NoRWX] Proof-of-Concept
purpose: Demonstrates execution of position-independent x86-64 machine code from read/write-only memory without requiring executable permissions.
description: NoRWX is a security research project that explores unconventional code execution techniques. It leverages hardware breakpoints (DR0/DR7 registers) combined with Vectored Exception Handling (VEH) to execute arbitrary machine code from memory regions that lack execute permissions, bypassing traditional VirtualProtect and VirtualAlloc calls. This proof-of-concept demonstrates that traditional memory protection mechanisms can be bypassed through creative use of CPU debugging features.
features: No executable memory required
Hardware breakpoint-based code execution (DR0/DR7 registers)
Vectored Exception Handler integration for breakpoint management
x86-64 instruction decoder and emulator supporting arithmetic, logic, control flow, and stack operations
Native Windows API call support from emulated code
Automatic breakpoint reinstatement during execution flow

title: [PIR] Position-Independent Runtime
purpose: A C++23 framework enabling position-independent code generation across multiple platforms and architectures.
description: PIR is a comprehensive C++23 framework designed for creating truly position-independent code that can run on multiple platforms without modification. It provides a complete runtime environment including cryptographic primitives, network protocols, and an embedded scripting language. The framework resolves all dependencies at runtime using platform-specific techniques like Windows PEB walking and Linux syscalls, with a three-layer architecture: Core (platform-independent primitives), Platform (OS-specific interfaces), and Runtime (high-level features like cryptography and networking).
features: Multi-platform (Windows, Linux, UEFI) and multi-architecture (i386, x86_64, armv7a, aarch64)
Compile-time string embedding without .rdata dependencies
Full cryptographic stack (SHA-256/384/512, ChaCha20-Poly1305, ECC)
TLS 1.3 with certificate verification, DNS, HTTP, WebSocket
PIL embedded scripting language
Three-layer architecture: Core, Platform, and Runtime modules
technologies: C++23, Windows PEB/NTAPI, Linux syscalls, UEFI, CMake
audience: Security researchers, systems programmers, embedded developers

title: [PIL] Position-Independent Language
purpose: A lightweight, dynamically typed scripting language embedded within the Position-Independent Runtime.
description: PIL (Position Independent Language) is a lightweight scripting language designed to function without C runtime dependencies or .rdata sections. It provides scripting capabilities in constrained environments like shellcode and UEFI firmware while maintaining full position-independence. The language includes a comprehensive standard library, file I/O, and network operations, all registered from C++ for minimal footprint optimized for embedded contexts.
features: Dynamic typing with six value types (nil, bool, number, string, array, function)
Lexical scoping with nested block support (max 32 levels)
Control flow structures (if/else, while, for loops, for-each)
First-class functions with recursion and parameter support
20+ standard library functions (print, len, str, type, abs, min, max, floor, ceil, etc.)
File I/O operations (read, write, append, binary modes)
Network operations (sockets, DNS, HTTP, WebSocket)
Multi-platform builds (Windows, Linux, UEFI across i386, x86_64, armv7a, aarch64)
audience: Security researchers, systems programmers, embedded developers

title: [EC] Armenian Encoding Converter
purpose: A utility for converting Armenian text between multiple encoding formats including ANSI, Unicode, and ArmSCII.
description: EC (Encoding Converter) is a specialized tool for handling the complex landscape of Armenian text encodings. It supports conversion between legacy encodings like ArmSCII-8 and modern Unicode, making it essential for working with historical Armenian documents. The tool includes batch processing capabilities, a command-line interface, and integrates with Microsoft Office documents for seamless workflow integration.
features: Supports multiple Armenian encodings
Batch conversion support
Word and Excel document converters (TXT/DOC/DOCX/XLS/XLSX)
Command-line interface for batch and individual file processing
GUI application (in development)
useCases: Converting legacy Armenian documents to Unicode
Batch processing of multilingual text files
Migrating old databases with Armenian content
